1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
4	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
5	import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}
120

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
import { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-ts'

import { GeneratorRegistry } from './registry'

export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

1	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}
104

content.content:
1	import { assertNever } from '@prisma/internals'
2	
3	import { BasicBuilder } from './BasicBuilder'
4	import { Writer } from './Writer'
5	
6	type StringifyOptions = {
7	  indentLevel?: number
8	  newLine?: 'none' | 'leading' | 'trailing' | 'both'
9	}
10	
11	export function stringify(builder: BasicBuilder, { indentLevel = 0, newLine = 'none' }: StringifyOptions = {}) {
12	  const str = new Writer(indentLevel, undefined).write(builder).toString()
13	  switch (newLine) {
14	    case 'none':
15	      return str
16	    case 'leading':
17	      return '\n' + str
18	    case 'trailing':
19	      return str + '\n'
20	    case 'both':
21	      return '\n' + str + '\n'
22	    default:
23	      assertNever(newLine, 'Unexpected value')
24	  }
25	}
26

1	import { TypeBuilder } from './TypeBuilder'
2	import { Writer } from './Writer'
3	
4	export class PrimitiveType extends TypeBuilder {
5	  constructor(private name: string) {
6	    super()
7	  }
8	
9	  write(writer: Writer): void {
10	    writer.write(this.name)
11	  }
12	}
13	
14	export const stringType = new PrimitiveType('string')
15	export const numberType = new PrimitiveType('number')
16	export const booleanType = new PrimitiveType('boolean')
17	export const nullType = new PrimitiveType('null')
18	export const undefinedType = new PrimitiveType('undefined')
19	export const bigintType = new PrimitiveType('bigint')
20	export const unknownType = new PrimitiveType('unknown')
21	export const anyType = new PrimitiveType('any')
22	export const voidType = new PrimitiveType('void')
23	export const thisType = new PrimitiveType('this')
24	export const neverType = new PrimitiveType('never')
25

1	import fs from 'node:fs'
2	import fsPromises from 'node:fs/promises'
3	import path from 'node:path'
4	import { stripVTControlCharacters } from 'node:util'
5	
6	import { omit } from '@prisma/client-common'
7	import { GeneratorRegistry, getGenerator, getPackedPackage, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
8	import { describe, expect, test, vi } from 'vitest'
9	
10	import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '../src/generator'
11	
12	function addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
13	  test,
14	  get,
15	  set,
16	}: {
17	  test: (value: unknown) => boolean
18	  get: (value: unknown) => string
19	  set: (value: unknown, sanitized: string) => unknown
20	}) {
21	  expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
22	    test: (val) => test(val) && (get(val).includes(__dirname) || get(val).includes('\\')),
23	    serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
24	      const newStr = get(val).replaceAll(__dirname, '/project').replaceAll('\\', '/')
25	      return printer(set(val, newStr), config, indentation, depth, refs)
26	    },
27	  })
28	}
29	
30	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
31	  test: (value: unknown) => typeof value === 'string',
32	  get: (val) => val as string,
33	  set: (_, sanitized) => sanitized,
34	})
35	
36	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
37	  test: (value: unknown) => value instanceof Error,
38	  get: (val) => (val as Error).message,
39	  set: (val, sanitized) => {
40	    const error = val as Error
41	    error.message = sanitized
42	    return error
43	  },
44	})
45	
46	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
47	  test(val) {
48	    if (typeof val !== 'object' || val == null) {
49	      return false
50	    }
51	    if (!('fromEnvVar' in val && 'native' in val && 'value' in val)) {
52	      return false
53	    }
54	    return val.native === true && val.value !== 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET'
55	  },
56	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
57	    const newVal = { ...val, value: 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET' }
58	    return printer(newVal, config, indentation, depth, refs)
59	  },
60	})
61	
62	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
63	  test: (val) => val instanceof Error && val.message.includes('\x1B'),
64	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
65	    val.message = stripVTControlCharacters((val as Error).message)
66	    return printer(val, config, indentation, depth, refs)
67	  },
68	})
69	
70	const registry = {
71	  'prisma-client-js': {
72	    type: 'in-process',
73	    generator: new PrismaClientJsGenerator(),
74	  },
75	} satisfies GeneratorRegistry
76	
77	describe('generator', () => {
78	  test('minimal', async () => {
79	    const prismaClientTarget = path.join(__dirname, './node_modules/@prisma/client')
80	    // Make sure, that nothing is cached.
81	    await fsPromises.rm(prismaClientTarget, { recursive: true, force: true })
82	    await getPackedPackage('@prisma/client', prismaClientTarget)
83	    await fsPromises.cp(path.join(__dirname, '../../client/runtime'), path.join(prismaClientTarget, 'runtime'), {
84	      recursive: true,
85	    })
86	
87	    if (!fs.existsSync(prismaClientTarget)) {
88	      throw new Error(`Prisma Client didn't get packed properly 🤔`)
89	    }
90

content.text: Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire generator ecosystem. Let me now launch a Plan agent to design the implementation:

input.prompt:
I need to design an implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema. Here's all the context I've gathered:

## Goal
Create `@prisma/rest-api-generator` — a new Prisma generator package that:
- For each model in the schema, generates a TypeScript interface representing the model
- For each model, generates typed CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
- Implements the standard Prisma generator protocol (the `Generator` interface)
- Outputs the generated TypeScript client to a user-specified output directory
- Follows the existing monorepo package structure exactly

## Key Architecture Facts

### Generator Protocol
- Generators implement `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`: `{ name: string, getManifest(config): Promise<GeneratorManifest>, generate(options): Promise<void> }`
- `GeneratorOptions` provides `dmmf: DMMF.Document` which has `datamodel.models: Model[]` — each model has `name`, `fields: Field[]`, etc.
- `Field` has `kind` ('scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported'), `name`, `type` (e.g. 'String','Int','DateTime'), `isRequired`, `isList`, `isId`, `isUnique`
- The generator also supports external (JSON-RPC) mode via `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`

### Existing Generator Packages
- `packages/client-generator-js/` — traditional generator, implements `Generator` interface
- `packages/client-generator-ts/` — newer TS generator, same pattern
- Both export their generator class from `src/index.ts`
- Both are registered in `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts`
- Both use `adapterConfig` build (bundled single entry CJS+ESM)

### Scalar Type Mapping (from client-generator-ts/src/utils/common.ts)
```typescript
const GraphQLScalarToJSTypeTable = {
  String: 'string',
  Int: 'number',
  Float: 'number',
  Boolean: 'boolean',
  Long: 'number',
  DateTime: ['Date', 'string'],
  ID: 'string',
  UUID: 'string',
  Json: 'JsonValue',
  Bytes: 'Buffer',
  Decimal: ['number', 'string'],
  BigInt: ['bigint', 'number'],
}
```

### TypeScript Builders (`@prisma/ts-builders`)
Fluent API for generating TypeScript code:
- `interfaceDeclaration(name)` → creates interface, `.add(property)`, `.addMultiple(properties)`
- `property(name, type)` → `.optional()`, `.readonly()`, `.setDocComment()`
- `file()` → `.addImport(import)`, `.add(declaration)`
- `moduleExport(declaration)` → wraps with `export`
- `stringify(builder)` → produces string output
- Primitive types: `stringType`, `numberType`, `booleanType`, `nullType`, `bigintType`, `anyType`, `voidType`
- `namedType(name)`, `unionType([types])`, `array(elementType)`

### Package Structure Pattern
Each package needs:
```
packages/<name>/
├── package.json     (name: "@prisma/<name>", scripts: dev/build/test/prepublishOnly)
├── tsconfig.json    (extends ../../tsconfig.json, include: ["./**/*"])
├── tsconfig.build.json  (extends ../../tsconfig.build.regular.json, outDir: dist, rootDir: src)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts     (imports build + adapterConfig from helpers/compile/)
└── src/
    ├── index.ts     (public exports)
    └── ...
```

### Files to also update:
1. `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path mapping: `"@prisma/rest-api-generator": ["./packages/rest-api-generator/src"]`
2. `packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` — register the new generator
3. `packages/client-generator-registry/package.json` — add `"@prisma/rest-api-generator": "workspace:*"` dependency
4. `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add to `client-packages` job

### DMMF Model Access
```typescript
// In generate():
const models = options.dmmf.datamodel.models
for (const model of models) {
  // model.name = "User", "Post", etc.
  // model.fields = [{name: "id", type: "Int", kind: "scalar", isRequired: true, isId: true}, ...]
  // model.fields with kind === 'object' are relations
}
```

## Design Requirements

The REST API client should generate code that looks roughly like this for a `User` model:

```typescript
export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
  posts: Post[]  // relation fields could be optional/excluded
}

// Input types for create/update
export interface UserCreateInput {
  email: string
  name?: string | null
  // exclude relation fields, auto-generated fields (isId + hasDefaultValue)
}

export interface UserUpdateInput {
  email?: string
  name?: string | null
}

// Where input for findUnique
export interface UserWhereUniqueInput {
  id?: number
  email?: string  // if @unique
}

// The REST client class
export class RestApiClient {
  constructor(private baseUrl: string) {}
  
  user = {
    findMany: async (params?: { /* filters */ }): Promise<User[]> => { ... },
    findUnique: async (where: UserWhereUniqueInput): Promise<User | null> => { ... },
    create: async (data: UserCreateInput): Promise<User> => { ... },
    update: async (where: UserWhereUniqueInput, data: UserUpdateInput): Promise<User> => { ... },
    delete: async (where: UserWhereUniqueInput): Promise<User> => { ... },
  }
}
```

Please design a detailed implementation plan. Consider:
1. File structure for the new package
2. How to map DMMF fields to TypeScript types (for the REST client, simpler than full Prisma client)
3. How to handle relations (skip them in create/update inputs, include in model interface)
4. How to identify unique fields for WhereUniqueInput
5. How to generate the REST client class with proper fetch-based implementation
6. Testing strategy — unit tests for the generator similar to existing patterns
7. Build and integration into the monorepo

Output a step-by-step plan with file paths and code structure.

1	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string
7	  output: EnvValue | null
8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81	
82	export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
83	
84	export type GeneratorManifest = {
85	  prettyName?: string
86	  defaultOutput?: string
87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
93	  version?: string
94	  requiresEngineVersion?: string
95	}

27	}>
28

30	  name: string

32	}>
33	
34	export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
35	  name: string

37	}>
38	
39	export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
40	  models: Model[]

46	export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
47	  name: string
48	  fields: string[]
49	}>

51	  name: string | null
52	  fields: string[]
53	}>
54	export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
55	  name: string
56	  dbName: string | null
57	  schema: string | null
58	  fields: Field[]
59	  uniqueFields: string[][]

63	  isGenerated?: boolean

66	export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
67	
68	export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
69	export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'
70	
71	export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
72	  kind: FieldKind
73	  name: string
74	  isRequired: boolean
75	  isList: boolean
76	  isUnique: boolean
77	  isId: boolean
78	  isReadOnly: boolean
79	  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
80	  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
81	  /**

83	   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
84	   */
85	  type: string
86	  /**
87	   * Native database type, if specified.
88	   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
89	   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
90	   */
91	  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
92	  dbName?: string | null
93	  hasDefaultValue: boolean
94	  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
95	  relationFromFields?: string[]
96	  relationToFields?: string[]
97	  relationOnDelete?: string
98	  relationOnUpdate?: string
99	  relationName?: string
100	  documentation?: string
101	}>
102

105	  args: Array<string | number>
106	}>
107

109	
110	export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{

115	  dbName?: string

117	  clustered?: boolean

131

1	import { TypeBuilder } from './TypeBuilder'
2	import { Writer } from './Writer'
3	
4	export class PrimitiveType extends TypeBuilder {
5	  constructor(private name: string) {
6	    super()
7	  }
8	
9	  write(writer: Writer): void {
10	    writer.write(this.name)
11	  }
12	}
13	
14	export const stringType = new PrimitiveType('string')
15	export const numberType = new PrimitiveType('number')
16	export const booleanType = new PrimitiveType('boolean')
17	export const nullType = new PrimitiveType('null')
18	export const undefinedType = new PrimitiveType('undefined')
19	export const bigintType = new PrimitiveType('bigint')
20	export const unknownType = new PrimitiveType('unknown')
21	export const anyType = new PrimitiveType('any')
22	export const voidType = new PrimitiveType('void')
23	export const thisType = new PrimitiveType('this')
24	export const neverType = new PrimitiveType('never')

1	import { assertNever } from '@prisma/internals'
2	
3	import { BasicBuilder } from './BasicBuilder'
4	import { Writer } from './Writer'
5	
6	type StringifyOptions = {
7	  indentLevel?: number
8	  newLine?: 'none' | 'leading' | 'trailing' | 'both'
9	}
10	
11	export function stringify(builder: BasicBuilder, { indentLevel = 0, newLine = 'none' }: StringifyOptions = {}) {
12	  const str = new Writer(indentLevel, undefined).write(builder).toString()
13	  switch (newLine) {
14	    case 'none':
15	      return str
16	    case 'leading':
17	      return '\n' + str
18	    case 'trailing':
19	      return str + '\n'
20	    case 'both':
21	      return '\n' + str + '\n'
22	    default:
23	      assertNever(newLine, 'Unexpected value')
24	  }
25	}

11	function addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
12	  test,
13	  get,
14	  set,
15	}: {
16	  test: (value: unknown) => boolean
17	  get: (value: unknown) => string
18	  set: (value: unknown, sanitized: string) => unknown
19	}) {
20	  expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
21	    test: (val) => test(val) && (get(val).includes(__dirname) || get(val).includes('\\')),
22	    serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
23	      const newStr = get(val).replaceAll(__dirname, '/project').replaceAll('\\', '/')
24	      return printer(set(val, newStr), config, indentation, depth, refs)
25	    },
26	  })
27	}
28	
29	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
30	  test: (value: unknown) => typeof value === 'string',
31	  get: (val) => val as string,
32	  set: (_, sanitized) => sanitized,
33	})
34	
35	addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
36	  test: (value: unknown) => value instanceof Error,
37	  get: (val) => (val as Error).message,
38	  set: (val, sanitized) => {
39	    const error = val as Error
40	    error.message = sanitized
41	    return error
42	  },
43	})
44	
45	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
46	  test(val) {
47	    if (typeof val !== 'object' || val == null) {
48	      return false
49	    }
50	    if (!('fromEnvVar' in val && 'native' in val && 'value' in val)) {
51	      return false
52	    }
53	    return val.native === true && val.value !== 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET'
54	  },
55	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
56	    const newVal = { ...val, value: 'NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET' }
57	    return printer(newVal, config, indentation, depth, refs)
58	  },
59	})
60	
61	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
62	  test: (val) => val instanceof Error && val.message.includes('\x1B'),
63	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
64	    val.message = stripVTControlCharacters((val as Error).message)
65	    return printer(val, config, indentation, depth, refs)
66	  },
67	})
68	
69	// Non-standard `AggregateError` from `p-map` (different from standard JS AggregateError).
70	// We flatten it into a new error with a single message containing all the error messages
71	// to avoid problems with stack traces in snapshots.
72	expect.addSnapshotSerializer({
73	  test: (val) => val instanceof Error && val.name === 'AggregateError' && typeof val[Symbol.iterator] === 'function',
74	  serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
75	    const error = val as Error & Iterable<Error>
76	    const newError = new Error([...error].map((e) => e.message).join('\n'))
77	    return printer(newError, config, indentation, depth, refs)
78	  },
79	})

5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',

44	    outdir: 'dist',

69	export function buildClient({
70	  schemaPath,
71	  runtimeBase,

74	  outputDir,

83	  generatedFileExtension,
84	  importFileExtension,
85	  moduleFormat,
86	  tsNoCheckPreamble,
87	  compilerBuild,
88	}: O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'>): BuildClientResult {
89	  // we define the basic options for the client generation
90	  const runtimeName = getRuntimeNameForTarget(target)
91	
92	  const outputName = generatedFileNameMapper(generatedFileExtension)
93	  const importName = importFileNameMapper(importFileExtension)
94	
95	  const clientOptions: TSClientOptions = {
96	    dmmf: getPrismaClientDMMF(dmmf),
97	    datasources,
98	    generator,
99	    binaryPaths,
100	    schemaPath,
101	    outputDir,
102	    runtimeBase,
103	    clientVersion,
104	    engineVersion,
105	    activeProvider,
106	    datamodel,
107	    edge: (['wasm-compiler-edge'] as RuntimeName[]).includes(runtimeName),
108	    runtimeName: runtimeName,
109	    target,
110	    generatedFileExtension,
111	    importFileExtension,
112	    moduleFormat,
113	    tsNoCheckPreamble,
114	    compilerBuild,
115	  }
116	
117	  const client = new TSClient(clientOptions)
118	
119	  let fileMap = client.generateClientFiles()
120	
121	  if (typedSql && typedSql.length > 0) {
122	    fileMap = {
123	      ...fileMap,
124	      ...buildTypedSql({
125	        dmmf,
126	        runtimeBase: getTypedSqlRuntimeBase(runtimeBase),
127	        runtimeName,
128	        queries: typedSql,
129	        outputName,
130	        importName,
131	      }),
132	    }
133	  }
134	
135	  fileMap = {
136	    ...fileMap,
137	    internal: {
138	      ...(fileMap.internal as FileMap),
139	      ...buildWasmFileMap({
140	        runtimeName,
141	        activeProvider,
142	        compilerBuild,
143	      }),
144	    },
145	  }
146	
147	  addPreambleToSourceFiles(fileMap, tsNoCheckPreamble)
148	
149	  return {
150	    fileMap, // a map of file names to their contents
151	    prismaClientDmmf: dmmf, // the DMMF document
152	  }
153	}

155	// relativizes runtime import base for typed sql
156	// absolute path stays unmodified, relative goes up a level
157	function getTypedSqlRuntimeBase(runtimeBase: string) {
158	  if (!runtimeBase.startsWith('.')) {
159	    // absolute path
160	    return runtimeBase
161	  }
162	
163	  if (runtimeBase.startsWith('./')) {
164	    // replace ./ with ../
165	    return `.${runtimeBase}`
166	  }
167	
168	  return `../${runtimeBase}`
169	}

173	    datamodel,

175	    generator,
176	    dmmf,
177	    datasources,
178	    binaryPaths,
179	    clientVersion,
180	    engineVersion,

184	    generatedFileExtension,
185	    importFileExtension,
186	    moduleFormat,
187	    tsNoCheckPreamble,
188	    compilerBuild,
189	  } = options
190	
191	  const { runtimeBase, outputDir } = await getGenerationDirs(options)
192	
193	  const { prismaClientDmmf, fileMap } = buildClient({
194	    datamodel,
195	    schemaPath,
196	    runtimeBase,
197	    outputDir,
198	    generator,
199	    dmmf,

200	    datasources,
201	    binaryPaths,
202	    clientVersion,
203	    engineVersion,
204	    activeProvider,
205	    typedSql,
206	    target,
207	    generatedFileExtension,
208	    importFileExtension,
209	    moduleFormat,

212	  })
213	
214	  const denylistsErrors = validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf)
215	
216	  if (denylistsErrors) {
217	    let message = `${bold(
218	      red('Error: '),
219	    )}The schema at "${schemaPath}" contains reserved keywords.\n       Rename the following items:`
220	
221	    for (const error of denylistsErrors) {
222	      message += '\n         - ' + error.message
223	    }
224	
225	    message += `\nTo learn more about how to rename models, check out https://pris.ly/d/naming-models`
226	
227	    throw new DenylistError(message)
228	  }
229	
230	  await deleteOutputDir(outputDir)
231	  await ensureDir(outputDir)
232	
233	  await writeFileMap(outputDir, fileMap)
234	}

236	function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {
237	  return Promise.all(
238	    Object.entries(fileMap).map(async ([fileName, content]) => {
239	      const absolutePath = path.join(outputDir, fileName)
240	      // The deletion of the file is necessary, so VSCode
241	      // picks up the changes.
242	      await fs.rm(absolutePath, { recursive: true, force: true })
243	      if (typeof content === 'string' || Buffer.isBuffer(content)) {
244	        // file
245	        await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)
246	      } else {
247	        // subdirectory
248	        await fs.mkdir(absolutePath)
249	        await writeFileMap(absolutePath, content)
250	      }
251	    }),
252	  )
253	}

3	export const INDENT_SIZE = 2
4	/**
5	 * Helper class for building long multi-line formatted strings from building blocks.
6	 * Can use either plain strings or `Builder` objects, that would encapsulate formatting logic.
7	 */
8	export class Writer<ContextType = undefined> {
9	  private lines: string[] = []
10	  private currentLine = ''
11	  private currentIndent = 0
12	  private marginSymbol?: string
13	  private afterNextNewLineCallback?: () => void
14	
15	  constructor(
16	    startingIndent = 0,
17	    readonly context: ContextType,
18	  ) {
19	    this.currentIndent = startingIndent
20	  }
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Adds provided value to the current line. Does not end the line.
24	   *
25	   * @param value
26	   * @returns
27	   */
28	  write(value: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>): this {
29	    if (typeof value === 'string') {
30	      this.currentLine += value
31	    } else {
32	      value.write(this)
33	    }
34	    return this
35	  }
36	
37	  /**
38	   * Adds several `values` to the current line, separated by `separator`. Both values and separator
39	   * can also be `Builder` instances for more advanced formatting.
40	   *
41	   * @param separator
42	   * @param values
43	   * @param writeItem allow to customize how individual item is written
44	   * @returns
45	   */
46	  writeJoined<T extends string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>>(
47	    separator: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>,
48	    values: T[],
49	    writeItem: (item: T, writer: this) => void = (item, w) => w.write(item),
50	  ): this {
51	    const last = values.length - 1
52	    for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
53	      writeItem(values[i], this)
54	      if (i !== last) {
55	        this.write(separator)
56	      }
57	    }
58	    return this
59	  }
60	
61	  /**
62	   * Adds a string to current line, flushes current line and starts a new line.
63	   * @param line
64	   * @returns
65	   */
66	  writeLine(line: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>): this {
67	    return this.write(line).newLine()
68	  }
69	
70	  /**
71	   * Flushes current line and starts a new line. New line starts at previously configured indentation level
72	   * @returns
73	   */
74	  newLine(): this {
75	    this.lines.push(this.indentedCurrentLine())
76	    this.currentLine = ''
77	    this.marginSymbol = undefined
78	
79	    const afterNextNewLineCallback = this.afterNextNewLineCallback
80	    this.afterNextNewLineCallback = undefined
81	    afterNextNewLineCallback?.()
82	    return this
83	  }
84	
85	  /**
86	   * Increases indentation level by 1, calls provided callback and then decreases indentation again.
87	   * Could be used for writing indented blocks of text:
88	   *
89	   * @example
90	   * ```ts
91	   * writer
92	   *   .writeLine('{')
93	   *   .withIndent(() => {
94	   *      writer.writeLine('foo: 123');
95	   *      writer.writeLine('bar: 456');
96	   *   })
97	   *   .writeLine('}')
98	   * ```
99	   * @param callback
100	   * @returns
101	   */
102	  withIndent(callback: (writer: this) => void): this {
103	    this.indent()
104	    callback(this)
105	    this.unindent()
106	    return this
107	  }
108	
109	  /**
110	   * Calls provided callback next time when new line is started.
111	   * Callback is called after old line have already been flushed and a new
112	   * line have been started. Can be used for adding "between the lines" decorations,
113	   * such as underlines.
114	   *
115	   * @param callback
116	   * @returns
117	   */
118	  afterNextNewline(callback: () => void) {
119	    this.afterNextNewLineCallback = callback
120	    return this
121	  }
122	
123	  /**
124	   * Increases indentation level of the current line by 1
125	   * @returns
126	   */
127	  indent(): this {
128	    this.currentIndent++
129	    return this
130	  }
131	
132	  /**
133	   * Decreases indentation level of the current line by 1, if it is possible
134	   * @returns
135	   */
136	  unindent(): this {
137	    if (this.currentIndent > 0) {
138	      this.currentIndent--
139	    }
140	    return this
141	  }
142	
143	  /**
144	   * Adds a symbol, that will replace the first character of the current line (including indentation)
145	   * when it is flushed. Can be used for adding markers to the line.
146	   *
147	   * Note: if indentation level of the line is 0, it will replace the first actually printed character
148	   * of the line. Use with caution.
149	   * @param symbol
150	   * @returns
151	   */
152	  addMarginSymbol(symbol: string): this {
153	    this.marginSymbol = symbol
154	    return this
155	  }
156	
157	  toString() {
158	    return this.lines.concat(this.indentedCurrentLine()).join('\n')
159	  }
160	
161	  getCurrentLineLength() {
162	    return this.currentLine.length
163	  }
164	
165	  private indentedCurrentLine(): string {
166	    const line = this.currentLine.padStart(this.currentLine.length + INDENT_SIZE * this.currentIndent)
167	    if (this.marginSymbol) {
168	      return this.marginSymbol + line.slice(1)
169	    }
170	    return line
171	  }
172	}
